text:
<think>I should start by calculating how much John earns in one day by multiplying his hourly wage by the number of hours he works per day.</think>
John earns $2 per hour and works 5 hours a day, so he makes $2 × 5 = $10 per day.
<think>Next, I need to determine how much he earns in one week by multiplying his daily earnings by the number of days he works per week.</think>
He works 4 days a week, so he earns $10 × 4 = $40 per week.
<think>Now, I should find out how many weeks it will take him to save $80 by dividing his savings goal by his weekly earnings.</think>
To save $80, it will take him $80 ÷ $40 = 2 weeks.
<think>I should now present the final numerical answer after the required #### marker.</think>
#### 2
